description.p: Starting at 1st level, you can harness your negative feelings into a usable source of energy, called cursed energy. These feelings can be harnessed into attacks or abilities. The number of cursed energy you possess is based on your highest level class. You have a curse energy stat equal to the highest among charisma and wisdom.
Your cursed energy DC is 8 + user cursed energy ability modifier + proficiency bonus.

sheet.description: You have {{ce:points:total}} Cursed Energy and your Cursed Energy DC is {{ce:dc:total}}. Your cursed energy stat is {{cu:stat}} (+{{ce:modifier}}).

description.p: After reaching 20 Cursed Energy score, you have learned to use Maximum Output techniques with the highest possible cursed energy channeled into them. When spending the most possible CE, you can spend half the cursed energy cost an additional time to amplify the output of your techniques, using one of the following benefits (rounded up):

ul.li: The technique's range is increased by half its original range rounded to the highest 5. For example, a technique with 10 feet of range would become a 15 feet range technique. This does not work with touch based techniques.
The technique's damage dice is increased by half of its original dice. For example, a technique with 10d10 damage die would add 5d10 die of damage to the total damage dice.
The technique's attack bonus is increased by half of its original bonus rounded up. For example, a technique with a +7 to hit would add a +4 to hit. DCs increase by half your proficiency bonus.
The technique's duration increase by half the original duration. Eg. techniques that last 1 minute now last 1 minute + 5 rounds.
Another value of your technique increases by half its original. If using Maximum Output on a shikigami summoning technique, you instead choose one of the following:
Increase the size category by 1 and the shikigami deals an additional damage dice in necrotic on a hit.
The shikigami's hit points increase by half its original value.
The shikigami's attack bonus increases by half your proficiency bonus (rounded up).

sheet.description: When spending the most possible CE on a technique, you can spend half the cursed energy cost an additional time to use one of the following benefits (rounded up): • The technique's range is increased by half its original range rounded to the highest 5. • The technique's damage dice is increased by half of its original dice. • The technique's attack bonus is increased by half of its original bonus rounded up. • The technique's duration increase by half the original duration. • Another value of your technique increases by half its original. If using Maximum Output on a shikigami summoning technique, you instead choose one of the following: • Increase the size category by 1 and the shikigami deals an additional damage dice in necrotic on a hit. • The shikigami's hit points increase by half its original value. • The shikigami's attack bonus increases by half your proficiency bonus (rounded up).

description.p: Wish whoosh, reverse cursed technique processes negative energy into positive energy by taking two sources of cursed energy and multiplying them by one other.

After almost dying, be it being reduced to 0 in one hit while at max hp, or failing two death saves and then rolling a nat 20. Those are moments where the GM can choose to bestow reverse cursed technique. Alternatively roll a d100, on a 90+ you get Reverse Cursed Technique.

You learn to use your cursed energy in a completely opposite way, channeling positive energy instead of negative. You have a pool of healing you can use with your reverse cursed energy that's equal to 5 x your level x your Cursed Energy modifier. As a bonus action for cursed energy up to your CE Modifier, you can remove healing from that pool to regain hit points. You can also remove healing from the pool as a reaction to receiving damage once per round. For every 1 cursed energy spent, you regain 10 hit points, removing an equal amount from the pool. If you are suffering from a physical condition or injury such as a concussion or something else, one of these effects can be healed for every 10 hit points recovered with the reverse cursed technique. Limbs can be regenerated for 20 hit points. You can use your Reverse Cursed Technique until your healing pool ends before getting burned out, during which you can't use RCT until your healing pool recovers. At the beginning of every two rounds, your healing pool regains 10 hit points. Your healing pool automatically refills at the end of a long rest.

sheet.description: You have a pool of healing you can use with your reverse cursed energy that's equal to 5 x {{level}} x {{ce:modifier}}. If you are suffering from a physical condition or injury such as a concussion or something else, one of these effects can be healed for every 10 hit points recovered with the reverse cursed technique. Limbs can be regenerated for 20 hit points. You can use your Reverse Cursed Technique until your healing pool ends before getting burned out, during which you can't use RCT until your healing pool recovers. At the beginning of every two rounds, your healing pool regains 10 hit points. Your healing pool automatically refills at the end of a long rest.

description.p: As a bonus action for cursed energy up to your CE Modifier, you can remove healing from that pool to regain hit points. You can also remove healing from the pool as a reaction to receiving damage once per round. For every 1 cursed energy spent, you regain 10 hit points, removing an equal amount from the pool.

sheet.description: For up to {{ce:modifier}} cursed energy, you can remove healing from that pool to regain hit points. For every 1 cursed energy spent, you regain {{rct:heal}} hit points, removing an equal amount from the pool.

description.p: All domains require a specific hand sign for their expansion to be activated, taking a Full Turn Action (Action + Bonus Action + Movement) to activate. All domains cost 20 Cursed Energy unless specified, and trap all creatures of your choice in a 175-foot circle centered on you, creating a barrier for 1 minute. The insides of a domain expansion vary from user to user, since it's like showing your soul to your opponents. Outside of your domain, its appearance is of a giant black sphere floating in the air.

All creatures outside your domain will receive Xd6 bludgeoning damage(X = CE modifier) and be pushed to the edge of the barrier. They received an additional 1d4 bludgeoning damage per object they collided with as the domain pushed them. In case the domain forces them against a wall, they must start holding their breath until the domain is closed or shrunk. Your barrier has domain hit points equal to 10 times your CE modifier and is vulnerable to damage from the outside and resistant to damage from the inside. Work with your GM to determine the environment and terrain of your domain.

Technique Burnout. Even if the barrier or the environment isn't fully formed, casting domain expansion will cause the user's innate technique to become unstable for a short time afterward. Right after ending your domain expansion, you cannot use any innate technique features and gain 2 levels of exhaustion for 1 minute or until you land a black flash.

sheet.description: For 20 cursed energy, trap all creatures of your choice in a 175-foot circle centered on you, creating a barrier for 1 minute. All creatures outside your domain take {{ce:modifier}}d6 bludgeoning damage and are pushed to the edge of the barrier. They received an additional 1d4 bludgeoning damage per object they collide with as the domain pushes them. If they are against a wall, they must start holding their breath until the domain is closed or shrunk. Your barrier has {{domain:expansion:hp}} hit points and is vulnerable to damage from the outside and resistant to damage from the inside.

description.p: You have obtained your 1ST Breakthrough (You must equip every single one). Events reached either during extreme focus or moments near death, you manage to glimpse the core of cursed energy. Breakthroughs take one of two forms.
Black Flash. Channeling cursed energy into an attack within 0.000001, you cause a spatial distortion where cursed energy sparks black. This event is called a black flash.

In order to land a black flash, you must land a critical hit. After landing a critical hit, you roll a second d20, on a natural 20 you activate a black flash. This causes the melee attack to deal triple critical damage, rolling 3x the dice instead of 2x and ignore damage resistances.
Reverse Curse Technique.Wish whoosh, reverse cursed technique processes negative energy into positive energy by taking two sources of cursed energy and multiplying them by one other.

After almost dying, be it being reduced to 0 in one hit while at max hp, or failing two death saves and then rolling a nat 20. Those are moments where the GM can choose to bestow reverse cursed technique. Alternatively roll a d100, on a 90+ you get Reverse Cursed Technique.

You learn to use your cursed energy in a completely opposite way, channeling positive energy instead of negative. You have a pool of healing you can use with your reverse cursed energy that's equal to 5 x your level x your Cursed Energy modifier. As a bonus action for cursed energy up to your CE Modifier, you can remove healing from that pool to regain hit points. You can also remove healing from the pool as a reaction to receiving damage once per round. For every 1 cursed energy spent, you regain 10 hit points, removing an equal amount from the pool. If you are suffering from a physical condition or injury such as a concussion or something else, one of these effects can be healed for every 10 hit points recovered with the reverse cursed technique. Limbs can be regenerated for 20 hit points.

You can use your Reverse Cursed Technique until your healing pool ends before getting burned out, during which you can't use RCT until your healing pool recovers. At the beginning of every two rounds, your healing pool regains 10 hit points. Your healing pool automatically refills at the end of a long rest.
You have obtained your 2ND Breakthrough. Events reached either during extreme focus or moments near death, you manage to glimpse the core of cursed energy. Breakthroughs take one of two forms.
Black Flash. Channeling cursed energy into an attack within 0.000001, you cause a spatial distortion where cursed energy sparks black. This event is called a black flash.

In order to land a black flash, you must land a critical hit. After landing a critical hit, you roll a second d20, on a natural 20 you activate a black flash. This causes the melee attack to deal triple critical damage, rolling 3x the dice instead of 2x and ignore damage resistances.
Reverse Curse Technique.Wish whoosh, reverse cursed technique processes negative energy into positive energy by taking two sources of cursed energy and multiplying them by one other.

After almost dying, be it being reduced to 0 in one hit while at max hp, or failing two death saves and then rolling a nat 20. Those are moments where the GM can choose to bestow reverse cursed technique. Alternatively roll a d100, on a 90+ you get Reverse Cursed Technique.

You learn to use your cursed energy in a completely opposite way, channeling positive energy instead of negative. You have a pool of healing you can use with your reverse cursed energy that's equal to 5 x your level x your Cursed Energy modifier. As a bonus action for cursed energy up to your CE Modifier, you can remove healing from that pool to regain hit points. You can also remove healing from the pool as a reaction to receiving damage once per round. For every 1 cursed energy spent, you regain 10 hit points, removing an equal amount from the pool. If you are suffering from a physical condition or injury such as a concussion or something else, one of these effects can be healed for every 10 hit points recovered with the reverse cursed technique. Limbs can be regenerated for 20 hit points.

You can use your Reverse Cursed Technique until your healing pool ends before getting burned out, during which you can't use RCT until your healing pool recovers. At the beginning of every two rounds, your healing pool regains 10 hit points. Your healing pool automatically refills at the end of a long rest.
You have obtained your 3RD Breakthrough. Events reached either during extreme focus or moments near death, you manage to glimpse the core of cursed energy. Breakthroughs take one of two forms.
Black Flash. Channeling cursed energy into an attack within 0.000001, you cause a spatial distortion where cursed energy sparks black. This event is called a black flash.

In order to land a black flash, you must land a critical hit. After landing a critical hit, you roll a second d20, on a natural 20 you activate a black flash. This causes the melee attack to deal triple critical damage, rolling 3x the dice instead of 2x and ignore damage resistances.
Reverse Curse Technique.Wish whoosh, reverse cursed technique processes negative energy into positive energy by taking two sources of cursed energy and multiplying them by one other.

After almost dying, be it being reduced to 0 in one hit while at max hp, or failing two death saves and then rolling a nat 20. Those are moments where the GM can choose to bestow reverse cursed technique. Alternatively roll a d100, on a 90+ you get Reverse Cursed Technique.

You can use your Reverse Cursed Technique until your healing pool ends before getting burned out, during which you can't use RCT until your healing pool recovers. At the beginning of every two rounds, your healing pool regains 10 hit points. Your healing pool automatically refills at the end of a long rest.
You have obtained your 4TH Breakthrough. Events reached either during extreme focus or moments near death, you manage to glimpse the core of cursed energy. Breakthroughs take one of two forms.
Black Flash. Channeling cursed energy into an attack within 0.000001, you cause a spatial distortion where cursed energy sparks black. This event is called a black flash.
